Google 


This  is  a  digital  copy  of  a  book  that  was  preserved  for  generations  on  library  shelves  before  it  was  carefully  scanned  by  Google  as  part  of  a  project 

to  make  the  world's  books  discoverable  online. 

It  has  survived  long  enough  for  the  copyright  to  expire  and  the  book  to  enter  the  public  domain.  A  public  domain  book  is  one  that  was  never  subject 

to  copyright  or  whose  legal  copyright  term  has  expired.  Whether  a  book  is  in  the  public  domain  may  vary  country  to  country.  Public  domain  books 

are  our  gateways  to  the  past,  representing  a  wealth  of  history,  culture  and  knowledge  that's  often  difficult  to  discover. 

Marks,  notations  and  other  maiginalia  present  in  the  original  volume  will  appear  in  this  file  -  a  reminder  of  this  book's  long  journey  from  the 

publisher  to  a  library  and  finally  to  you. 

Usage  guidelines 

Google  is  proud  to  partner  with  libraries  to  digitize  public  domain  materials  and  make  them  widely  accessible.  Public  domain  books  belong  to  the 
public  and  we  are  merely  their  custodians.  Nevertheless,  this  work  is  expensive,  so  in  order  to  keep  providing  tliis  resource,  we  liave  taken  steps  to 
prevent  abuse  by  commercial  parties,  including  placing  technical  restrictions  on  automated  querying. 
We  also  ask  that  you: 

+  Make  non-commercial  use  of  the  files  We  designed  Google  Book  Search  for  use  by  individuals,  and  we  request  that  you  use  these  files  for 
personal,  non-commercial  purposes. 

+  Refrain  fivm  automated  querying  Do  not  send  automated  queries  of  any  sort  to  Google's  system:  If  you  are  conducting  research  on  machine 
translation,  optical  character  recognition  or  other  areas  where  access  to  a  large  amount  of  text  is  helpful,  please  contact  us.  We  encourage  the 
use  of  public  domain  materials  for  these  purposes  and  may  be  able  to  help. 

+  Maintain  attributionTht  GoogXt  "watermark"  you  see  on  each  file  is  essential  for  in  forming  people  about  this  project  and  helping  them  find 
additional  materials  through  Google  Book  Search.  Please  do  not  remove  it. 

+  Keep  it  legal  Whatever  your  use,  remember  that  you  are  responsible  for  ensuring  that  what  you  are  doing  is  legal.  Do  not  assume  that  just 
because  we  believe  a  book  is  in  the  public  domain  for  users  in  the  United  States,  that  the  work  is  also  in  the  public  domain  for  users  in  other 
countries.  Whether  a  book  is  still  in  copyright  varies  from  country  to  country,  and  we  can't  offer  guidance  on  whether  any  specific  use  of 
any  specific  book  is  allowed.  Please  do  not  assume  that  a  book's  appearance  in  Google  Book  Search  means  it  can  be  used  in  any  manner 
anywhere  in  the  world.  Copyright  infringement  liabili^  can  be  quite  severe. 

About  Google  Book  Search 

Google's  mission  is  to  organize  the  world's  information  and  to  make  it  universally  accessible  and  useful.   Google  Book  Search  helps  readers 
discover  the  world's  books  while  helping  authors  and  publishers  reach  new  audiences.  You  can  search  through  the  full  text  of  this  book  on  the  web

INTRODUCTION 


I  have  come  to  regard  a  book  as  curtailed  of  half  its  value  if  it  has 
not  a  pretty  full  index. — Binney. 

For  a  work  of  such  wide  scope  as  The  Library  of 
Universal  Literature  a  comprehensive  index  is  in- 
dispensable. Though  the  work  is  encyclopedic  in 
character,  and  for  this  reason  arranged  alphabetically 
for  convenient  reference  to  authors,  it  is  more  than 
an  encyclopedia  of  writers.  Were  the  articles  limited 
to  biographies  this  alphabetical  arrangement  would 
answer  the  purpose  of  the  most  exacting  student. 

But  in  the  compilation  of  this  work  the  editor  has 
done  more.  He  has  classified  the  writers  according 
to  their  works,  made  selections  from  their  best  books, 
and  culled  opinions  from  able  contemporary  and 
subsequent  critics  to  show  their  relative  merits. 
Many  who  are  known  to  the  world  in  general  as 
romancists  have,  on  the  close  scrutiny  to  which  they 
have  been  subjected  during  the  compilation  of  this 
work,  been  found  to  have  produced  poetry,  critical 
essays,  or  contributions  to  science  or  history  which 
might  have  made  them  famous  in  those  lines  had  not 
their  greater  works  overshadowed  others  of  real  merit. 
Many  a  noted  philosopher  might  be  considered  a 
great  wit  could  we  but  lose  sight  of  his  sage  sayings. 
In  classifying  the  various  authors  it  has  therefore 
been  found  necessary  frequently  to  place  the  same 
2  name  under  several  headings,  so  the  inquirer  may 
5       not  be  at  a  loss  to  find  the  name  of  a  poet  under  the 


7> 


3- 


2  INTRODUCTION' 

proper  classification,  even  though  he  also  be  known 
as  a  novelist.  This  section  of  the  index  will  be  found 
of  great  value  to  anyone  searching  for  writers  in  the 
several  departments  of  literature. 

The  collection  of  excerpts  from  the  principal  works 
of  the  authors  represented  would  lose  more  than  half 
its  value  without  being  indexed.  It  will  be  found  by 
reference  to  this  index  that  many  subjects  are  treated 
of  by  different  authors  in  different  parts  of  the  work, 
and  where  one  might  scarcely  expect  to  find  them  with- 
out such  reference.  The  selections  also  comprise  m  uch 
biographical  matter  and  critical  opinions  of  authors 
not  found  in  the  regular  alphabetical  order.  For  the 
purpose  of  compiling  theses,  essays  or  sermons,  or  for 
strengthening  one*s  own  opinions  by  getting  the 
matured  thoughts  of  others  on  the  same  questions,  it 
will  be  readily  seen  that  this  section  of  the  index  is 
invaluable. 

Though  the  title  of  each  extract  has  been  written 
with  a  view  to  suggesting  the  tenor  of  the  article,  it 
frequently  transpires  that  the  heading  (often  the 
author's  own  and  selected  at  his  caprice)  indicates 
neither  the  character  of  the  writing  nor  the  subject 
treated.  The  present  editor  thought  it  expedient 
therefore  to  further  set  forth  the  titles  to  the  extracts 
in  an  Analytical  Index,  wherein  the  selections  are 
grouped  under  several  general  headings,  to  indicate 
either  the  subject-matter  or  the  rhetorical  character 
of  the  writings.  In  making  this  analysis  of  the  selec- 
tions, the  poetry,  in  which  the  work  is  exceptionally 

« 

rich,  has  been  grouped  into  verses  suitable  for  quota- 
tion on  various  occasions  and  on  the  subjects  usually 
sung  of  by  the  bard,  such  as  "Love,"  "Patriotism,*' 
"  The  Sea,"  "  Death,"  etc.     It  is  hoped  the  need  of 


IxWTRODUCTIO^r  3 

ready  reference  to  appropriate  quotations  will  be  filled 
by  this  section  of  the  Index. 

The  Index  of  Bibh'ography  is  a  compendium  of  the 
world's  literature.  What  studious  reader  has  not  felt 
the  necessity  of  such  a  work !  It  is  evident  that  to 
read  all  the  works  on  one  subject  (unless  it  be  a 
trifling  one)  is  impossible,  even  for  a  specialist.  But 
by  reference  to  this  Bibliographical  Index  the  choice 
of  several  may  be  made,  and  possibly  one  by  an 
author  acknowledged  to  be  the  master  in  his  line. 

Description  of  the  Index  is  not  complete  without 
a  brief  reference  to  the  synchronistic  charts.  As 
chronology  is  the  framework  of  history,  so  synchro- 
nology  is  the  skeleton  of  literature.  The  value  of 
many  a  book  is  determined  by  the  circumstances 
under  which  it  was  written.  A  careful  study  of  these 
tables  will  disclose  the  gradual  unrolling  of  the  scroll 
of  literature  in  the  light  of  civil  and  political  history, 
as  well  as  the  effect  on  literature  of  the  world  s 
progress.
